perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, certain investors have agreed to provide financing (the "Financing") to Pathnet, Inc. (the "Company") subject to the terms of that certain Investment and Stockholders' Agreement, dated October 31, 1997 (the "Investment and Stockholders' Agreement"), by and among the Company, David Schaeffer and the investors identified therein (the "Investors"); WHEREAS, such Financing will significantly benefit the Company and indirectly benefit the above-named Employee, as a stockholder of the Company; and WHEREAS, this Non-Disclosure, Assignment of Inventions and Non-Competition Agreement (this "Agreement") is a condition to the Investment and Stockholders' Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, certain investors have agreed to provide financing (the "Financing") to Pathnet, Inc. (the "Company"); WHEREAS, such Financing will significantly benefit the Company and indirectly benefit the above-named Employee, as a stockholder of the Company; and WHEREAS, this Non-Disclosure, Assignment of Inventions and Non-Competition Agreement (this "Agreement") is a condition to the Financing.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'subject to the terms of that certain Investment and Stockholders' Agreement, dated October 31, 1997 (the "Investment and Stockholders' Agreement"), by and among the Company, David Schaeffer and the investors identified therein (the "Investors")' from the first WHEREAS clause makes the condition vague. Later, in Section 14, 'This Agreement is being entered into at the request of the Investors and such Investors are intended to be third-party beneficiaries hereunder with full power to enforce the terms hereof.' lacks clear reference to the Investment and Stockholders' Agreement, creating an ambiguity as to the specific rights of the 'Investors' mentioned in section 14.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is being entered into at the request of the Investors and such Investors are intended to be third-party beneficiaries hereunder with full power to enforce the terms hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly links the 'Financing' to the 'Investment and Stockholders' Agreement' and the investors identified therein. The changed text removes this link, making the condition vague. The contradicted text states that 'This Agreement is being entered into at the request of the Investors', and the investors are intended to be third-party beneficiaries. By removing the explicit reference to the 'Investment and Stockholders' Agreement', the 'Investors' mentioned in the contradicted text lack clear reference, which creates an ambiguity as to the specific rights. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is created.

perturbation.original_text: I agree that I will promptly disclose to the Company any and all such Inventions, and that, upon request of the Company, I will execute and deliver any and all documents or instruments and take any other action which the Company shall deem necessary to assign to and vest completely in the Company, to perfect trademark, copyright and patent protection with respect to, or to otherwise protect the Company's trade secrets and proprietary interest in such Inventions.
perturbation.changed_text: I agree that I will promptly disclose to the Company any and all such Inventions, and that, upon request of the Company, I will execute and deliver any and all documents or instruments and take any other action which the Company shall deem necessary to assign to and vest completely in the Company, to perfect trademark protection with respect to, or to otherwise protect the Company's trade secrets and proprietary interest in such Inventions.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'copyright and patent' after 'trademark' in Section 2, the requirement to deliver documents to perfect 'copyright and patent protection' is removed. However, the earlier statement in Section 2 stating 'ASSIGNMENT OF INVENTIONS. I expressly understand and agree that any and all right or interest I have or obtain in any designs, trade secrets, technical specifications, technical data, know-how and show-how, internal reports and memoranda, marketing plans, inventions, concepts, ideas, expressions, discoveries, improvements, copyrights, and patent or patent rights conceived' still refers to copyrights and patents, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ASSIGNMENT OF INVENTIONS. I expressly understand and agree that any and all right or interest I have or obtain in any designs, trade secrets, technical specifications, technical data, know-how and show-how, internal reports and memoranda, marketing plans, inventions, concepts, ideas, expressions, discoveries, improvements, copyrights, and patent or patent rights conceived, devised, developed, reduced to practice, or which I otherwise have or obtain during the term of this Agreement which relates to the business of the Company or arise out of my employment with the Company are expressly regarded as "works for hire" (the "Inventions").
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'copyright and patent' from the list of protections the employee must help the company perfect via document delivery. The contradicted text, 'ASSIGNMENT OF INVENTIONS,' explicitly includes 'copyrights, and patent or patent rights' as Inventions assigned to the company. By removing the requirement to perfect copyright and patent protection, the changed text contradicts the clause assigning those rights to the company, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the employee is no longer obligated to help perfect the copyright and patent protection.

perturbation.original_text: All documents, records, apparatus, equipment and other physical property, whether or not pertaining to Confidential Information, which are furnished to me by the Company or are produced by me in connection with my employment will be and remain the sole property of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: All documents, records, equipment and other physical property, whether or not pertaining to Confidential Information, which are furnished to me by the Company or are produced by me in connection with my employment will be and remain the sole property of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'apparatus' in Section 3, it implies that apparatus are not the sole property of the Company. However, Section 3 continues with 'I will return to the Company all such materials and property as and when requested by the Company.' This section includes 'apparatus' in its implication, hence creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All documents, records, equipment and other physical property, whether or not pertaining to Confidential Information, which are furnished to me by the Company or are produced by me in connection with my employment will be and remain the sole property of the Company. I will return to the Company all such materials and property as and when requested by the Company. In any event, I will return all such materials and property immediately upon termination of my employment for any reason. I will not take with me any such material or property or any copies thereof upon such termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text included 'apparatus' in the list of items that remain the sole property of the company. The changed text omits 'apparatus'. The contradicted text states 'All documents, records, equipment and other physical property...will be and remain the sole property of the Company' and further specifies 'I will return to the Company all such materials and property'. By removing 'apparatus' in the changed text, it creates a contradiction. The changed text suggests apparatus is not the sole property of the company, while the contradicted text implies it is subject to being returned to the Company, thus creating an in-text contradiction as described by the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: While I am employed by the Company and for a period of two (2) years after the termination or cessation of such employment for any reason, I shall not, without the Company's prior written consent, directly or indirectly, alone or as a partner, joint venturer, officer, director, employee, consultant, agent, independent contractor or stockholder of any company or business, engage in any business activity which is or may reasonably be construed to be competitive with the "PathNet Business."
perturbation.changed_text: While I am employed by the Company and for a period of two (2) years after the termination or cessation of such employment for any reason, I shall not, without the Company's prior written consent, directly or indirectly, alone or as a partner, joint venturer, officer, director, employee, consultant, agent, independent contractor or stockholder of any company or business, engage in any business activity which may be competitive with the "PathNet Business."
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'is or may reasonably be construed to be' before 'competitive' in Section 4, the language becomes less specific. The phrase 'reasonably be construed to be' sets a lower threshold for what is considered competitive. This omission now creates a contradiction in the sentence right after that which says:'For purposes of this Agreement, the "PathNet Business" shall mean the business of installing, constructing, aggregating and linking digital capacity and marketing and selling the bulk telecommunications capacity and services created by such systems.' The Pathnet Business is defined in broad terms that would have been easily be construed to be competitive, but without the omission, it limits what can be considered competative.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-COMPETITION COVENANT. While I am employed by the Company and for a period of two (2) years after the termination or cessation of such employment for any reason, I shall not, without the Company's prior written consent, directly or indirectly, alone or as a partner, joint venturer, officer, director, employee, consultant, agent, independent contractor or stockholder of any company or business, engage in any business activity which may be competitive with the "PathNet Business." For purposes of this Agreement, the "PathNet Business" shall mean the business of installing, constructing, aggregating and linking digital capacity and marketing and selling the bulk telecommunications capacity and services created by such systems. My ownership of not more than one(1%) percent of the shares of any class of stock of any corporation which is actively traded on a national securities exchange or on NASDAQ shall not be deemed, in and of itself, to violate the prohibitions of this paragraph. I understand that the restrictions set forth in this Section are intended to protect the Company's valid business interests and agree that such restrictions are reasonable and appropriate for this purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text used the phrase "is or may reasonably be construed to be competitive". By omitting "is or may reasonably be construed to be", the changed text narrows the scope of what is considered 'competitive'. The 'PathNet Business' definition is broad ('installing, constructing, aggregating and linking digital capacity and marketing and selling the bulk telecommunications capacity and services created by such systems.'). With the original broader language, a wider range of activities could be deemed competitive. By removing the phrase 'is or may reasonably be construed to be', the change introduces a contradiction because it now requires the activity to actually 'be' competitive and not just 'construed' to be competitive which therefore limits what is 'competitive' based on the broad definition of pathnet business.

perturbation.original_text: During my employment with the Company and for a period two (2) years thereafter, I will not encourage any employee of the Company to terminate their employment with the Company, nor solicit nor hire any employee of the Company for employment by any corporation or any other commercial enterprise other than the Company, unless first approached in writing by the Company employee.
perturbation.changed_text: During my employment with the Company and for a period two (2) years thereafter, I will not encourage any employee of the Company to terminate their employment with the Company, nor solicit nor hire any employee of the Company for employment by any corporation or any other commercial enterprise other than the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'unless first approached in writing by the Company employee' from Section 5 creates a stricter non-solicitation clause. This contradicts the sentence that says 'I understand that the restrictions set forth in this Section are intended to protect the Company's valid business interests and agree that such restrictions are reasonable and appropriate for this purpose.' Now, it is considered a blanket ban on any employee of the company to terminate, hence, not reasonable nor appropriate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-SOLICITATION. During my employment with the Company and for a period two (2) years thereafter, I will not encourage any employee of the Company to terminate their employment with the Company, nor solicit nor hire any employee of the Company for employment by any corporation or any other commercial enterprise other than the Company. I understand that the restrictions set forth in this Section are intended to protect the Company's valid business interests and agree that such restrictions are reasonable and appropriate for this purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for solicitation if the employee first approached the individual in writing. The changed text removes this exception, creating a stricter non-solicitation clause. The contradicted text includes the sentence 'I understand that the restrictions set forth in this Section are intended to protect the Company's valid business interests and agree that such restrictions are reasonable and appropriate for this purpose.' By removing the exception, the agreement becomes a blanket ban, which might be considered unreasonable, thereby contradicting the reasonableness assertion within the contradicted text. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: If I am terminated for any reason, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I will receive a salary of $275,000 per annum for one year after such termination payable in bi-weekly installments in accordance with the Company's payroll procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: If I am terminated for any reason, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I will receive a salary for one year after such termination payable in bi-weekly installments in accordance with the Company's payroll procedures.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the amount of the salary ('$275,000 per annum') in Section 9, the value becomes undefined, contradicting the purpose of the severance clause. Section 10 states, 'I agree that it would be difficult to measure any damages caused to the Company which might result from any breach by me of the promises set forth in this Agreement, and that, in any event, money damages would be an inadequate remedy for any such breach.' Without the salary amount being stated, it is hard to tell if the money damages would be inadequate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REMEDIES UPON BREACH. I agree that it would be difficult to measure any damages caused to the Company which might result from any breach by me of the promises set forth in this Agreement, and that, in any event, money damages would be an inadequate remedy for any such breach. Accordingly, I agree that if I breach or propose to breach any portion of this Agreement, the Company shall be entitled, in addition to all other remedies that it may have, to an injunction or other appropriate equitable relief to restrain any such breach without showing or proving any actual damage to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a salary of '$275,000 per annum' in the severance clause. The changed text omits this amount, making the salary undefined. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which refers to 'money damages' being an inadequate remedy upon breach. Without a defined salary, it becomes difficult to determine if the money damages are in fact inadequate, creating a strong contradiction and uncertainty within the contract regarding the value of the severance and its adequacy as a remedy.
